# Conventions

[//]: # 'This section is autogenerated, but the following list order, title, and [Tag]s can be modified, but nothing within the parentheses.'

<PagesIndex>

- Markdown First - ([Outline](#markdown-first), [Contents](./markdown-first/page.mdx))

## Markdown First

Every documentation file should be useful as a static markdown document
before any React components or interactivity are added.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Human-First Authoring
  - Why Markdown
  - Progressive Enhancement
  - Do / Don't
  - Images and Media
  - Autogenerated Files
  - Related Conventions

</details>

## Semantic HTML

All useful content should be represented in the HTML without JavaScript.
This ensures crawlers, screen readers, and users on slow connections
can access documentation content immediately.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Code Blocks
  - Type Tables
  - Testing by Disabling JavaScript
  - Related

</details>

## File Navigation

A consistent file structure makes it easy to find what you need.
The patterns below apply across the repository.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Quick Reference
  - Source Files
    - Export Definitions
    - Function Source
    - Component Source
    - Hook Source
  - Documentation Files
    - Page
    - Page Index
    - Type Reference
    - Type Config
    - Demos
  - Tests
  - Page Metadata
  - Following Imports
    - Navigating Without Prior Knowledge
  - Related Conventions

</details>

## Fragment Delimiters

We use `:` to express hierarchy inside URL fragments. Example: `https://example.com/page#section:subsection` uses `:` to separate a primary section (`section`) from a nested part (`subsection`). The full value appears in an element's `id`, e.g. `section:subsection`.

<details>

<summary>Outline</summary>

- Sections:
  - Rationale
  - Syntax
  - Examples
  - Do / Don't
  - Interoperability
    - History Behavior
    - Text Fragment Compatibility
  - Accessibility
  - HTML `id` Safety
  - Implementation (Conceptual)
  - Future Considerations
  - FAQ
  - References

</details>
